    "About": [
      "それはコロンビアのカトリック司祭であり革命家であるカミロ・トーレス・レストレポの教義と遺産についてです。"

    "HasA": [
      "それはキリスト教とマルクス主義の思想の融合を持っています。",
      "それは「新しいキリスト教」への信念を持っています。",
      "それは革命の段階的構想の拒否を持っています。",
      "それは正戦の教義に基づく武力革命の正当化を持っています。",
      "それは伝統的な左翼的立場を再考する提案を持っています。",
      "それはキリスト教徒が貧しい人々を助ける義務を果たすための正当な方法としての武力革命の立場を持っています。",

      "それは貧しい多数派が権力を掌握する革命への願望を持っています。",
      "それはキリスト教徒にとって義務であるという信念を持っています。",
      "それは少数派が武力抵抗をしない場合に平和的な革命への願望を持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは革命的キリスト教によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはカトリック革命的社会主義によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはキリスト教とマルクス主義の思想の融合によって特徴づけられます。",

      "それはローマ・カトリック教会が社会の改革において積極的な役割を果たすことによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは革命がキリスト教徒にとって義務であるという信念によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは教会財産の収用によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは宣教的司牧プログラムによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは学校におけるカトリックの排他的教育の放棄によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは自己犠牲としての愛の強調によって特徴づけられます。"

About: It is about the doctrine and legacy of Colombian Catholic priest and revolutionary Camilo Torres Restrepo.
Affects: It affects the reformation of society.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Christian and Marxist thought.
It is associated with Christians for Socialism.
It is associated with the Sandinistas.
It is associated with the Montoneros.
It is associated with the United Front of the People.
It is associated with liberation theology.
BelongsTo: It belongs to Catholic revolutionary socialist political ideologies.
ConsistsOf: It consists of Christian and Marxist thought.
CreatedBy: It was created by Camilo Torres Restrepo.
CreatedTo: It was created to provide a solution to Colombia's problems.
HasA: It has a fusion of Christian and Marxist thought.
It has a belief in a "new Christianity".
It has a rejection of stage-based conceptions of revolution.
It has a justification for violent revolution based on the doctrine of just war.
It has a proposal of rethinking traditional leftist positions.
It has a position that armed revolution is a legitimate way for Christians to help the poor.
It has a position that revolution leads to unity of Christians and Marxists.
It has a desire for a revolution where the poor majorities seize power.
It has a belief that revolution is obligatory for Christians.
It has a desire for a peaceful revolution if minorities do not offer violent resistance.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by revolutionary Christianity.
It is characterized by Catholic revolutionary socialism.
It is characterized by a fusion of Christian and Marxist thought.
It is characterized by the doctrine and legacy of Camilo Torres Restrepo.
It is characterized by the Roman Catholic Church taking an active role in the reformation of society.
It is characterized by a belief that revolution is obligatory for Christians.
It is characterized by the expropriation of church property.
It is characterized by a missionary pastoral program.
It is characterized by the abandonment of exclusive teaching of Catholicism in schools.
It is characterized by the emphasis on love as surrender of self.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Cuban Revolution.
It was influenced by Fidel Castro.
It was influenced by Che Guevara's thesis on the construction of the new man in Cuba.
IsA: It is a Catholic revolutionary socialist political ideology.
It is a fusion of Christian and Marxist thought.
It is a precursor of liberation theology.
It is a predecessor of liberation theology.
KnownAs: It is known as Thought of Camilo.
It is known as Revolutionary Christianity.
KnownFor: It is known for its fusion of Christian and Marxist thought.
It is known for its revolutionary socialist political ideology.
It is known for its adoption by the Montoneros.
It is known for its belief in a "new Christianity".
It is known for its advocacy for the expropriation of church property.
It is known for its similarity to liberation theology.
NamedAfter: It is named after Camilo Torres Restrepo.
UsedFor: It is used for the reformation of society.
It is used for the improvement of the socioeconomic situation.
It is used for achieving a government that feeds the hungry, clothes the naked, and teaches the ignorant.
It is used for achieving love for all.
It is used for the struggle of Christians and Marxists.
UsedIn: It is used in revolutionary movements.
It is used in Latin America.
UsedTo: It is used to fulfill the duty of helping the poor.
It is used to achieve unity of the people.
It is used to seize power.
